coverage.claim: Draft-spring age-adjusted PPG percentile maps monotonically to WAR7 (~3x top-vs-bottom tercile) in every adequately-sampled league/position cell, 2011-2019, and adds value over the real draft order only in rounds 2-7. Ranks include measured market-mispricing tilts (residual study, 1,711 picks): small forwards and Jul-Dec-born forwards out-returned their slots; bigger defensemen without elite production under-returned theirs.

disclaimers: Rounds 2-7 are where this board beats the draft: top-decile draft-spring producers taken in rounds 2-7 hit (WAR7 >= 2) 44% of the time vs 7% expected from their slot (+1.12 WAR7 over slot expectation).
Round 1 is consensus territory: production adds nothing conditional on a round-1 grade -- do not use this board to re-order first-rounders.

NCAA rows (USCHO) carry no birth dates -- college players board only when a prior CHL/USHL roster proves their birth window -- and NCAA/MHL translation cells are thin, so those projections lean on pooled cells (league-thin flag). Remaining holes are on the watchlist.
Scoring-only: the signal is PPG percentile, blind to defense, skating, and role -- Anthony Cirelli is the canonical miss this method can never catch.
F and D are different scales: defensive value is invisible to a production board, so the global rank buries every defenseman. Read defensemen by their position rank (D #4), never the overall number.
Small flagship sample: the 44%-vs-7% rounds-2-7 cell is n=16 top-decile picks -- treat the overlay as a tilt, not a law.

Ranks fold in residual-study tilts (2011-2019, n>=115 per cell except one thin D cell capped at its neighbour): F size (+0.07 small / -0.15 big), F birth quarter (Jul-Sep +0.22 vs Jan-Mar -0.15 -- the calendar relative-age bias), and -0.24 for bigger D below the top production tercile. Unknown height/birthdate contributes zero, never a guess.
watchlist: Russians OUTSIDE the MHL (KHL/VHL regulars, rare at 17) -- MHL is fully covered with per-player birth dates; the senior leagues are not

NCAA freshmen with NO prior covered-league roster (rare) -- USCHO carries no birth dates, so eligibility rides last season's CHL/USHL roster; a player who skipped those leagues entirely cannot prove his birth window
Swedish J20 production -- rosters (DOB/size) imported; the game-sheet stats backfill is running and J20 scorers join the board as it lands
